cause_analysis.narrative: Sanctuary Spa NYC churned on 2026-03-03 after 126 days with Zoca. Sentiment turned negative on 2025-12-16 (sms) — first complaint keyword: "issue". Classification: explicit cancellation. The client formally asked to cancel; the question is whether a save attempt was made. 7 inbound message(s) went unanswered for 5+ days — this is the clearest operational failure on Zoca's side. Median Zoca response time was 7.0 days, above the 24-hour expectation.
recommendations: [Retention] Cancellation was signalled 4 day(s) before churn via email. Trigger an AM save call within 4 hours of any cancellation keyword — not a support response.
[Offer] Build a 3-tier retention offer ladder (pause / discount / scope change) so AMs have something concrete to counter with.
[SLA] 7 inbound message(s) went unanswered for 5+ days. Enforce a hard rule: every inbound gets a human response within 24h, every day of the week.

[SLA example] On 2025-12-11 via email, client wrote: "Subject: Accepted: Carmen DeBartolo-Kelly and Tanvi @ Tue Dec 16, 2025 1:30am - 2am (IST) (tanvi.m@zoca.com)\n\nBody: 96…" — no outbound response in 43 days.

[SLA example] On 2025-12-16 via email, client wrote: "Subject: Accepted: Zoca X Sanctuary Spa NYC @ Tue Dec 23, 2025 1:30am - 2am (IST) (tanvi.m@zoca.com)\n\nBody: 96 Zoca X …" — no outbound response in 38 days.

If you're interested, just reply ‘YES’ and we’ll chat here. If not, reply ‘NO’, no hard feelings at all (I will sip my coffee and will try to move on)
